lockfile-only churn is excluded by the smell floor itself
package.json edit with NO dependency change (scripts tweak) — dep-change smell needs depKeys
40-line deletion is under the legacy-deletion floor (100)
patch-level version bump of an existing dep — the canonical false positive the downgrade judge exists for
major bump of a devDependency test runner — a version change, not a road-not-taken
minor plugin bump alongside lock churn — still just a version change
regenerated file sync (AUTO-GENERATED header, mechanical churn) — trips the deletion floor but is routine
behavior-preserving move (identical content, new folder) trips module-replace — a refactor, not a decision

-export const startSocketServer = (httpServer: Server) => {
-  /* … */
-};
diff --git a/src/client/updates.ts b/src/client/updates.ts
--- a/src/client/updates.ts
+++ b/src/client/updates.ts
@@ -1,12 +1,8 @@
-import { subscribe } from './socket';
-export const watchEntity = (id: string, cb: (e: Entity) => void) =>
-  subscribe(`entity:${id}`, cb);
+// Realtime push removed: clients poll every 30s instead. Live updates were used by
+// <2% of sessions and the socket tier was our top infra cost.
+export const watchEntity = (id: string, cb: (e: Entity) => void) =>
+  setInterval(async () => cb(await fetchEntity(id)), 30_000);
note: removes the realtime subsystem in favour of polling — a reversal of a prior architectural bet

+const store = new Map<string, { v: unknown; exp: number }>();
+export const cache = {
+  get: (k: string) => { /* … */ },
+  set: (k: string, v: unknown, ttlMs = 60_000) => { /* … */ },
+};
note: shared cache tier → per-instance cache: same basename would be too easy; different name, same replacement decision with an accepted trade-off
moves auth enforcement across the client/server trust boundary — a security architecture decision
